title: Intense Savory Egyptian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Egyptian dish made with lamb, clotted cream, and spices, this tagine is a hearty and flavorful one-pot meal, with a resinous and snappy flavor profile that is diabetic-friendly.
instructions: Heat the oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the lardons and cook until crispy, then remove from the pot and set aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the garlic and ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb to the pot and cook until it is browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Add the clotted cream to the pot and stir to combine, then bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, until the lamb is tender, about 1 1/2 hours.
Stir in the crispy lardons and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
